message.content: CRIE UM FLUXOGRAMA DE COMO FUNCIONA A NANO TECHOLOGOGIA

content.thinking:
O usuário está pedindo para criar um fluxograma de como funciona a nanotecnologia. Ele quer que eu:
1. Gere apenas um arquivo
2. O arquivo deve conter apenas código Mermaid
3. Retorne apenas o path do arquivo com o código Mermaid

O usuário mencionou usar @skills\mermaid-diagram\SKILL.md para boas práticas. Vou primeiro ler esse arquivo para entender as boas práticas.

content.text: Vou criar um fluxograma detalhado sobre como funciona a nanotecnologia seguindo as melhores práticas do skill de diagramas Mermaid. Primeiro, deixe-me consultar as diretrizes de boas práticas:

1→---
     2→name: mermaid-diagram
     3→description: Create and modify Mermaid diagrams following best practices and avoiding common errors. Integrates with the meirmaid.py module for rendering. Automatically triggers when user asks to create, modify, or work with diagrams.
     4→allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep
     5→---
     6→
     7→# IMPORTANT
     8→
     9→this skill will be actived ONLY by the subagent meirmaid-diagram-builder (C:\Users\JOSE\.claude\agents\meirmaid-diagram-builder.md)
    10→
    11→# Mermaid Diagram Design
    12→
    13→Specialized skill for creating and modifying Mermaid diagrams with best practices and error prevention.
    14→
    15→## When to Use
    16→
    17→- User asks to create a diagram (flowchart, sequence, class, ER, etc.)
    18→- User asks to modify or update an existing diagram
    19→- User mentions visualization, schema, architecture, or structure
    20→- File has `.mermaid` or `.mmd` extension
    21→- Working with the meirmaid.py module
    22→
    23→## Mermaid Best Practices
    24→
    25→### 1. **Always Add Direction Markers**
    26→
    27→For flowcharts, always specify direction:
    28→
    29→```mermaid
    30→%% GOOD
    31→flowchart LR
    32→  A[Start] --> B[Process]
    33→
    34→%% GOOD
    35→flowchart TD
    36→  A[Start] --> B[Process]
    37→
    38→%% BAD - no direction
    39→graph
    40→  A --> B
    41→```
    42→
    43→### 2. **Use Consistent Naming Conventions**
    44→
    45→- Use `PascalCase` for nodes: `UserProcess`, `DatabaseHandler`
    46→- Use `snake_case` for IDs: `user_process`, `db_handler`
    47→- Keep names short but descriptive (max 20-30 chars)
    48→
    49→### 3. **Label Nodes Clearly**
    50→
    51→Always provide clear labels in brackets:
    52→
    53→```mermaid
    54→%% GOOD
    55→A[User Authentication]
    56→
    57→%% OK for simple cases
    58→B[Auth]
    59→
    60→%% BAD - unclear
    61→A[UA]
    62→```
    63→
    64→### 4. **Group Related Elements**
    65→
    66→Use subgraphs for organization:
    67→
    68→```mermaid
    69→flowchart TB
    70→  subgraph Frontend ["Client Layer"]
    71→    A[UI] --> B[API Client]
    72→  end
    73→
    74→  subgraph Backend ["Server Layer"]
    75→    C[Controller] --> D[Service]
    76→  end
    77→
    78→  B --> C
    79→```
    80→
    81→### 5. **Use Appropriate Arrow Styles**
    82→
    83→- `-->` : Standard flow
    84→- `==>` : Bold/main flow
    85→- `-.->` : Dotted/optional flow
    86→- `~~~` : Inverse flow
    87→
    88→### 6. **Add Comments for Clarity**
    89→
    90→```mermaid
    91→%% Authentication flow diagram
    92→%% Shows login process and error handling
    93→flowchart TB
    94→  %% Start point
    95→  A([Start]) --> B{Valid?}
    96→```
    97→
    98→## Common Errors to Avoid
    99→
   100→### ❌ **Syntax Errors**
   101→
   102→1. **Missing closing brackets**
   103→   ```mermaid
   104→   %% BAD
   105→   A[Open node
   106→
   107→   %% GOOD
   108→   A[Closed node]
   109→   ```
   110→
   111→2. **Invalid characters in IDs**
   112→   ```mermaid
   113→   %% BAD - spaces in ID
   114→   node 1 --> node 2
   115→
   116→   %% GOOD - spaces in label only
   117→   node1[node 1] --> node2[node 2]
   118→   ```
   119→
   120→3. **Unmatched parentheses in subgraphs**
   121→   ```mermaid
   122→   %% BAD
   123→   subgraph Name
   124→     A --> B
   125→
   126→   %% GOOD
   127→   subgraph Name
   128→     A --> B
   129→   end
   130→   ```
   131→
   132→### ❌ **Structure Errors**
   133→
   134→1. **Missing direction indicator in flowcharts**
   135→   ```mermaid
   136→   %% BAD
   137→   flowchart
   138→     A --> B
   139→
   140→   %% GOOD
   141→   flowchart LR
   142→     A --> B
   143→   ```
   144→
   145→2. **Self-referencing nodes without clear notation**
   146→   ```mermaid
   147→   %% Use loop notation instead of unclear self-references
   148→   A -->|retry| A
   149→   ```
   150→
   151→3. **Overlapping arrows without labels**
   152→   ```mermaid
   153→   %% BAD - unclear which path to take
   154→   A --> B
   155→   A --> B
   156→
   157→   %% GOOD - labeled paths
   158→   A -->|success| B
   159→   A -->|retry| B
   160→   ```
   161→
   162→### ❌ **Visual Clarity Errors**
   163→
   164→1. **Too many nodes in one diagram** (>20 nodes = consider splitting)
   165→2. **Lines too long** (use intermediate nodes)
   166→3. **Inconsistent styling** (stick to one style per diagram type)
   167→
   168→## Diagram Type Selection
   169→
   170→Choose the right diagram type for your purpose:
   171→
   172→| Purpose | Diagram Type | Example |
   173→|---------|--------------|---------|
   174→| Process flow | `flowchart` | Business logic, algorithms |
   175→| Time sequence | `sequenceDiagram` | API calls, user interactions |
   176→| Class structure | `classDiagram` | OOP design, database models |
   177→| Database schema | `erDiagram` | Table relationships |
   178→| State transitions | `stateDiagram-v2` | State machines, workflows |
   179→| Timeline | `gantt` | Project schedules, milestones |
   180→| Hierarchy | `pie` or `mindmap` | Distributions, categories |
   181→
   182→## Integration with meirmaid.py
   183→
   184→The mermaid module supports:
   185→- **Extensions**: `.mermaid`, `.mmd`
   186→- **Render mode**: Diagram with Mermaid.js
   187→- **Edit support**: Yes (via webhook)
   188→- **Input type**: Text descriptions
   189→
   190→### Working with meirmaid.py
   191→
   192→When creating diagrams for the meirmaid module:
   193→
   194→1. **Use proper file extensions** (`.mermaid` or `.mmd`)
   195→2. **Add descriptive headers** as comments
   196→3. **Keep diagrams self-contained** (no external dependencies)
   197→4. **Test basic syntax** before saving
   198→
   199→## Diagram Creation Workflow
   200→
   201→### Step 1: Understand Requirements
   202→
   203→Ask clarifying questions:
   204→- What type of diagram? (flowchart, sequence, etc.)
   205→- What's the main process/system being shown?
   206→- Who is the audience? (technical, business, mixed)
   207→- Any specific style preferences?
   208→
   209→### Step 2: Draft Structure
   210→
   211→1. List main components/nodes
   212→2. Identify relationships/flows
   213→3. Group related elements
   214→4. Plan the layout (LR, TD, etc.)
   215→
   216→### Step 3: Create Diagram
   217→
   218→1. Start with type declaration and direction
   219→2. Add nodes with clear labels
   220→3. Connect with appropriate arrows
   221→4. Add subgraphs if needed
   222→5. Include comments for context
   223→
   224→### Step 4: Validate
   225→
   226→Check for:
   227→- ✅ Syntax correctness
   228→- ✅ Clear node labels
   229→- ✅ Logical flow
   230→- ✅ Consistent styling
   231→- ✅ Appropriate complexity
   232→
   233→## Common Diagram Patterns
   234→
   235→### Authentication Flow
   236→
   237→```mermaid
   238→flowchart TB
   239→    A([User]) --> B{Credentials?}
   240→    B -->|Valid| C[Generate Token]
   241→    B -->|Invalid| D[Show Error]
   242→    C --> E[Access Granted]
   243→    D --> A
   244→```
   245→
   246→### API Request/Response
   247→
   248→```mermaid
   249→sequenceDiagram
   250→    participant Client
   251→    participant API
   252→    participant DB
   253→
   254→    Client->>API: POST /login
   255→    API->>DB: Query user
   256→    DB-->>API: User data
   257→    API-->>Client: JWT token
   258→```
   259→
   260→### Microservices Architecture
   261→
   262→```mermaid
   263→flowchart TB
   264→    subgraph Ingress
   265→        LB[Load Balancer]
   266→    end
   267→
   268→    subgraph Services
   269→        S1[Auth Service]
   270→        S2[User Service]
   271→        S3[Payment Service]
   272→    end
   273→
   274→    subgraph Data
   275→        DB1[(Users DB)]
   276→        DB2[(Payments DB)]
   277→    end
   278→
   279→    LB --> S1
   280→    LB --> S2
   281→    LB --> S3
   282→    S1 --> DB1
   283→    S2 --> DB1
   284→    S3 --> DB2
   285→```
   286→
   287→## Styling Guidelines
   288→
   289→### For Technical Diagrams
   290→- Use precise, technical terms
   291→- Include error handling paths
   292→- Show data flows clearly
   293→- Add decision diamonds for logic
   294→
   295→### For Business Diagrams
   296→- Use business language
   297→- Focus on processes and actors
   298→- Simplify technical details
   299→- Highlight key decision points
   300→
   301→### For Mixed Audiences
   302→- Balance technical and business terms
   303→- Add legends if needed
   304→- Use descriptive labels
   305→- Include glossary for acronyms
   306→
   307→## Troubleshooting
   308→
   309→### Diagram Won't Render
   310→
   311→1. **Check syntax**: Validate brackets, quotes, parentheses
   312→2. **Verify diagram type**: Ensure correct type declaration
   313→3. **Check for special characters**: Escape or remove problematic chars
   314→4. **Test incrementally**: Start simple, add complexity gradually
   315→
   316→### Diagram Looks Messy
   317→
   318→1. **Reduce node count**: Split into multiple diagrams
   319→2. **Adjust direction**: Try LR vs TD
   320→3. **Use subgraphs**: Group related elements
   321→4. **Shorten labels**: Long labels clutter the view
   322→
   323→### Arrow Confusion
   324→
   325→1. **Label all arrows**: Make flows explicit
   326→2. **Use different styles**: Distinguish main vs alternate flows
   327→3. **Add color**: Use styles to highlight paths (if supported)
   328→4. **Reduce crossings**: Reorganize nodes to minimize arrow intersections
   329→
   330→## Quality Checklist
   331→
   332→Before finalizing a diagram:
   333→
   334→- [ ] Syntax is valid (no render errors)
   335→- [ ] All nodes have clear, descriptive labels
   336→- [ ] Arrows are labeled and make logical sense
   337→- [ ] Direction is specified (LR/TD for flowcharts)
   338→- [ ] Diagram type matches the purpose
   339→- [ ] Complexity is appropriate (<20 nodes ideally)
   340→- [ ] Comments explain context
   341→- [ ] File extension is correct (.mermaid or .mmd)
   342→- [ ] Works with meirmaid.py module
   343→
   344→## Related Resources
   345→
   346→- [Mermaid Official Docs](https://mermaid.js.org/)
   347→- [Mermaid Live Editor](https://mermaid.live/) - Test diagrams online
   348→- @meirmaid.py - Local rendering module
   349→
   350→## Version History
   351→
   352→- **v1.0.0** (2025-03-09): Initial release with best practices and error prevention
   353→
   354→---
   355→
   356→## Last Updated
   357→
   358→**Date:** 2025-03-09

file.content:
---
name: mermaid-diagram
description: Create and modify Mermaid diagrams following best practices and avoiding common errors. Integrates with the meirmaid.py module for rendering. Automatically triggers when user asks to create, modify, or work with diagrams.
allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep
---

# IMPORTANT

this skill will be actived ONLY by the subagent meirmaid-diagram-builder (C:\Users\JOSE\.claude\agents\meirmaid-diagram-builder.md)

# Mermaid Diagram Design

Specialized skill for creating and modifying Mermaid diagrams with best practices and error prevention.

## When to Use

- User asks to create a diagram (flowchart, sequence, class, ER, etc.)
- User asks to modify or update an existing diagram
- User mentions visualization, schema, architecture, or structure
- File has `.mermaid` or `.mmd` extension
- Working with the meirmaid.py module

## Mermaid Best Practices

### 1. **Always Add Direction Markers**

For flowcharts, always specify direction:

```mermaid
%% GOOD
flowchart LR
  A[Start] --> B[Process]

%% GOOD
flowchart TD
  A[Start] --> B[Process]

%% BAD - no direction
graph
  A --> B
```

### 2. **Use Consistent Naming Conventions**

- Use `PascalCase` for nodes: `UserProcess`, `DatabaseHandler`
- Use `snake_case` for IDs: `user_process`, `db_handler`
- Keep names short but descriptive (max 20-30 chars)

### 3. **Label Nodes Clearly**

Always provide clear labels in brackets:

```mermaid
%% GOOD
A[User Authentication]

%% OK for simple cases
B[Auth]

%% BAD - unclear
A[UA]
```

### 4. **Group Related Elements**

Use subgraphs for organization:

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph Frontend ["Client Layer"]
    A[UI] --> B[API Client]
  end

  subgraph Backend ["Server Layer"]
    C[Controller] --> D[Service]
  end

  B --> C
```

### 5. **Use Appropriate Arrow Styles**

- `-->` : Standard flow
- `==>` : Bold/main flow
- `-.->` : Dotted/optional flow
- `~~~` : Inverse flow

### 6. **Add Comments for Clarity**

```mermaid
%% Authentication flow diagram
%% Shows login process and error handling
flowchart TB
  %% Start point
  A([Start]) --> B{Valid?}
```

## Common Errors to Avoid

### ❌ **Syntax Errors**

1. **Missing closing brackets**
   ```mermaid
   %% BAD
   A[Open node

   %% GOOD
   A[Closed node]
   ```

2. **Invalid characters in IDs**
   ```mermaid
   %% BAD - spaces in ID
   node 1 --> node 2

   %% GOOD - spaces in label only
   node1[node 1] --> node2[node 2]
   ```

3. **Unmatched parentheses in subgraphs**
   ```mermaid
   %% BAD
   subgraph Name
     A --> B

   %% GOOD
   subgraph Name
     A --> B
   end
   ```

### ❌ **Structure Errors**

1. **Missing direction indicator in flowcharts**
   ```mermaid
   %% BAD
   flowchart
     A --> B

   %% GOOD
   flowchart LR
     A --> B
   ```

2. **Self-referencing nodes without clear notation**
   ```mermaid
   %% Use loop notation instead of unclear self-references
   A -->|retry| A
   ```

3. **Overlapping arrows without labels**
   ```mermaid
   %% BAD - unclear which path to take
   A --> B
   A --> B

   %% GOOD - labeled paths
   A -->|success| B
   A -->|retry| B
   ```

### ❌ **Visual Clarity Errors**

1. **Too many nodes in one diagram** (>20 nodes = consider splitting)
2. **Lines too long** (use intermediate nodes)
3. **Inconsistent styling** (stick to one style per diagram type)

## Diagram Type Selection

Choose the right diagram type for your purpose:

| Purpose | Diagram Type | Example |
|---------|--------------|---------|
| Process flow | `flowchart` | Business logic, algorithms |
| Time sequence | `sequenceDiagram` | API calls, user interactions |
| Class structure | `classDiagram` | OOP design, database models |
| Database schema | `erDiagram` | Table relationships |
| State transitions | `stateDiagram-v2` | State machines, workflows |
| Timeline | `gantt` | Project schedules, milestones |
| Hierarchy | `pie` or `mindmap` | Distributions, categories |

## Integration with meirmaid.py

The mermaid module supports:
- **Extensions**: `.mermaid`, `.mmd`
- **Render mode**: Diagram with Mermaid.js
- **Edit support**: Yes (via webhook)
- **Input type**: Text descriptions

### Working with meirmaid.py

When creating diagrams for the meirmaid module:

1. **Use proper file extensions** (`.mermaid` or `.mmd`)
2. **Add descriptive headers** as comments
3. **Keep diagrams self-contained** (no external dependencies)
4. **Test basic syntax** before saving

## Diagram Creation Workflow

### Step 1: Understand Requirements

Ask clarifying questions:
- What type of diagram? (flowchart, sequence, etc.)
- What's the main process/system being shown?
- Who is the audience? (technical, business, mixed)
- Any specific style preferences?

### Step 2: Draft Structure

1. List main components/nodes
2. Identify relationships/flows
3. Group related elements
4. Plan the layout (LR, TD, etc.)

### Step 3: Create Diagram

1. Start with type declaration and direction
2. Add nodes with clear labels
3. Connect with appropriate arrows
4. Add subgraphs if needed
5. Include comments for context

### Step 4: Validate

Check for:
- ✅ Syntax correctness
- ✅ Clear node labels
- ✅ Logical flow
- ✅ Consistent styling
- ✅ Appropriate complexity

## Common Diagram Patterns

### Authentication Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    A([User]) --> B{Credentials?}
    B -->|Valid| C[Generate Token]
    B -->|Invalid| D[Show Error]
    C --> E[Access Granted]
    D --> A
```

### API Request/Response

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant API
    participant DB

    Client->>API: POST /login
    API->>DB: Query user
    DB-->>API: User data
    API-->>Client: JWT token
```

### Microservices Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    subgraph Ingress
        LB[Load Balancer]
    end

    subgraph Services
        S1[Auth Service]
        S2[User Service]
        S3[Payment Service]
    end

    subgraph Data
        DB1[(Users DB)]
        DB2[(Payments DB)]
    end

    LB --> S1
    LB --> S2
    LB --> S3
    S1 --> DB1
    S2 --> DB1
    S3 --> DB2
```

## Styling Guidelines

### For Technical Diagrams
- Use precise, technical terms
- Include error handling paths
- Show data flows clearly
- Add decision diamonds for logic

### For Business Diagrams
- Use business language
- Focus on processes and actors
- Simplify technical details
- Highlight key decision points

### For Mixed Audiences
- Balance technical and business terms
- Add legends if needed
- Use descriptive labels
- Include glossary for acronyms

## Troubleshooting

### Diagram Won't Render

1. **Check syntax**: Validate brackets, quotes, parentheses
2. **Verify diagram type**: Ensure correct type declaration
3. **Check for special characters**: Escape or remove problematic chars
4. **Test incrementally**: Start simple, add complexity gradually

### Diagram Looks Messy

1. **Reduce node count**: Split into multiple diagrams
2. **Adjust direction**: Try LR vs TD
3. **Use subgraphs**: Group related elements
4. **Shorten labels**: Long labels clutter the view

### Arrow Confusion

1. **Label all arrows**: Make flows explicit
2. **Use different styles**: Distinguish main vs alternate flows
3. **Add color**: Use styles to highlight paths (if supported)
4. **Reduce crossings**: Reorganize nodes to minimize arrow intersections

## Quality Checklist

Before finalizing a diagram:

- [ ] Syntax is valid (no render errors)
- [ ] All nodes have clear, descriptive labels
- [ ] Arrows are labeled and make logical sense
- [ ] Direction is specified (LR/TD for flowcharts)
- [ ] Diagram type matches the purpose
- [ ] Complexity is appropriate (<20 nodes ideally)
- [ ] Comments explain context
- [ ] File extension is correct (.mermaid or .mmd)
- [ ] Works with meirmaid.py module

## Related Resources

- [Mermaid Official Docs](https://mermaid.js.org/)
- [Mermaid Live Editor](https://mermaid.live/) - Test diagrams online
- @meirmaid.py - Local rendering module

## Version History

- **v1.0.0** (2025-03-09): Initial release with best practices and error prevention

---

## Last Updated

**Date:** 2025-03-09
**Model:** claude-sonnet-4-6

content.thinking:
Agora entendi as boas práticas para criar diagramas Mermaid. Vou criar um fluxograma detalhado sobre nanotecnologia seguindo essas diretrizes:

1. Usar flowchart com direção TD (top-down) ou TB (top-bottom)
2. Nomes claros e descritivos em PascalCase
3. IDs em snake_case
4. Subgrupos para organizar conceitos relacionados
5. Setas apropriadas com labels
6. Comentários explicativos
7. Máximo de 20 nós por diagrama

Vou criar um fluxograma abrangente sobre nanotecnologia cobrindo:
- Escalas de tamanho
- Métodos de produção
- Aplicações principais
- Propriedades únicas
- Áreas de atuação

content.text: Perfeito! Agora vou criar um fluxograma detalhado sobre nanotecnologia seguindo as melhores práticas:

input.content:
%% Diagrama de Fluxo: Funcionamento da Nanotecnologia
%% Mostra as escalas, métodos, propriedades e aplicações da nanotecnologia
%% Autor: Claude Sonnet
%% Data: 2026-03-14

flowchart TB
    %% Definição inicial
    Start([Nanotecnologia]) --> Scale{Escala Nanométrica}

    %% Escalas de tamanho
    Scale -->|1-100 nm| NanoScale["Escala Nanométrica"]
    Scale -->|< 1 nm| Atomic["Escala Atômica"]
    Scale -->|> 100 nm| Micro["Escala Microscópica"]

    %% Propriedades únicas
    NanoScale --> Properties["Propriedades Únicas"]
    Properties --> Prop1["Alta Razão Superfície/Volume"]
    Properties --> Prop2["Efeitos Quânticos"]
    Properties --> Prop3["Propriedades Ópticas Especiais"]
    Properties --> Prop4["Condutividade Elétrica Única"]
    Properties --> Prop5["Força Mecânica Superior"]

    %% Métodos de fabricação
    Properties --> Methods["Métodos de Fabricação"]

    subgraph TopDown["Abordagem Top-Down"]
        Top1["Litografia"]
        Top2["Usinagem a Laser"]
        Top3["Gravação Química"]
    end

    subgraph BottomUp["Abordagem Bottom-Up"]
        Bottom1["Auto-montagem Molecular"]
        Bottom2["Síntese Química"]
        Bottom3["Deposição de Vapor"]
    end

    Methods --> TopDown
    Methods --> BottomUp

    %% Materiais nanotecnológicos
    Bottom1 --> Materials["Materiais Nano"]
    Materials --> Mat1["Nanotubos de Carbono"]
    Materials --> Mat2["Graphene"]
    Materials --> Mat3["Nanopartículas Metálicas"]
    Materials --> Mat4["Pontos Quânticos"]
    Materials --> Mat5["Nanofios"]

    %% Aplicações por setor
    Mat1 --> Applications["Áreas de Aplicação"]

    subgraph Medicina["Medicina"]
        Med1["Drug Delivery"]
        Med2["Imagem Médica"]
        Med3["Terapia Gênica"]
        Med4["Biossensores"]
        Med5["Engenharia Tecidual"]
    end

    subgraph Eletronica["Eletrônica"]
        Elec1["Transistores Nanoscópicos"]
        Elec2["Memórias de Alta Densidade"]
        Elec3["Displays Flexíveis"]
        Elec4["Circuitos Quânticos"]
    end

    subgraph Energia["Energia"]
        Energ1["Células Solares"]
        Energ2["Baterias de Alta Capacidade"]
        Energ3["Hidrogênio Armazenamento"]
        Energ4["Super Capacitores"]
    end

    subgraph Ambiente["Meio Ambiente"]
        Amb1["Filtragem de Água"]
        Amb2["Remediação de Solo"]
        Amb3["Detecção de Poluentes"]
        Amb4["Catálise Verde"]
    end

    subgraph Industria["Indústria"]
        Ind1["Materiais Compósitos"]
        Ind2["Revestimentos Protetores"]
        Ind3["Lubrificantes Avançados"]
        Ind4["Têxteis Inteligentes"]
    end

    Applications --> Medicina
    Applications --> Eletronica
    Applications --> Energia
    Applications --> Ambiente
    Applications --> Industria

    %% Desafios e considerações
    Medicina --> Challenges["Desafios e Considerações"]
    Eletronica --> Challenges
    Energia --> Challenges
    Ambiente --> Challenges
    Industria --> Challenges

    Challenges --> Challenge1["Segurança e Toxicidade"]
    Challenges --> Challenge2["Regulamentação"]
    Challenges --> Challenge3["Ética"]
    Challenges --> Challenge4["Custo de Produção"]
    Challenges --> Challenge5["Escala Industrial"]

    %% Futuro
    Challenges --> Future["Futuro da Nanotecnologia"]
    Future --> Fut1["Nanomedicina Personalizada"]
    Future --> Fut2["Computação Quântica"]
    Future --> Fut3["Nanofábricas Moleculares"]
    Future --> Fut4["Novos Materiais"]

    %% Estilização
    classDef mainStyle fill:#2E86AB,stroke:#023E8A,color:#fff,stroke-width:3px
    classDef scaleStyle fill:#A23B72,stroke:#721c45,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px
    classDef propStyle fill:#F18F01,stroke:#c25500,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px
    classDef methodStyle fill:#C73E1D,stroke:#8a260e,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px
    classDef materialStyle fill:#3B1F2B,stroke:#2a1520,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px
    classDef appStyle fill:#6B705C,stroke:#3d4036,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px
    classDef challengeStyle fill:#D08C60,stroke:#9c5c36,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px
    classDef futureStyle fill:#56CFE1,stroke:#2a9db5,color:#1a3a40,stroke-width:2px

    class Start mainStyle
    class Scale,NanoScale,Atomic,Micro scaleStyle
    class Properties,Prop1,Prop2,Prop3,Prop4,Prop5 propStyle
    class Methods,TopDown,BottomUp methodStyle
    class Materials,Mat1,Mat2,Mat3,Mat4,Mat5 materialStyle
    class Applications,Medicina,Eletronica,Energia,Ambiente,Industria appStyle
    class Challenges,Challenge1,Challenge2,Challenge3,Challenge4,Challenge5 challengeStyle
    class Future,Fut1,Fut2,Fut3,Fut4 futureStyle

toolUseResult.content:
%% Diagrama de Fluxo: Funcionamento da Nanotecnologia
%% Mostra as escalas, métodos, propriedades e aplicações da nanotecnologia
%% Autor: Claude Sonnet
%% Data: 2026-03-14

flowchart TB
    %% Definição inicial
    Start([Nanotecnologia]) --> Scale{Escala Nanométrica}

    %% Escalas de tamanho
    Scale -->|1-100 nm| NanoScale["Escala Nanométrica"]
    Scale -->|< 1 nm| Atomic["Escala Atômica"]
    Scale -->|> 100 nm| Micro["Escala Microscópica"]

    %% Propriedades únicas
    NanoScale --> Properties["Propriedades Únicas"]
    Properties --> Prop1["Alta Razão Superfície/Volume"]
    Properties --> Prop2["Efeitos Quânticos"]
    Properties --> Prop3["Propriedades Ópticas Especiais"]
    Properties --> Prop4["Condutividade Elétrica Única"]
    Properties --> Prop5["Força Mecânica Superior"]

    %% Métodos de fabricação
    Properties --> Methods["Métodos de Fabricação"]

    subgraph TopDown["Abordagem Top-Down"]
        Top1["Litografia"]
        Top2["Usinagem a Laser"]
        Top3["Gravação Química"]
    end

    subgraph BottomUp["Abordagem Bottom-Up"]
        Bottom1["Auto-montagem Molecular"]
        Bottom2["Síntese Química"]
        Bottom3["Deposição de Vapor"]
    end

    Methods --> TopDown
    Methods --> BottomUp

    %% Materiais nanotecnológicos
    Bottom1 --> Materials["Materiais Nano"]
    Materials --> Mat1["Nanotubos de Carbono"]
    Materials --> Mat2["Graphene"]
    Materials --> Mat3["Nanopartículas Metálicas"]
    Materials --> Mat4["Pontos Quânticos"]
    Materials --> Mat5["Nanofios"]

    %% Aplicações por setor
    Mat1 --> Applications["Áreas de Aplicação"]

    subgraph Medicina["Medicina"]
        Med1["Drug Delivery"]
        Med2["Imagem Médica"]
        Med3["Terapia Gênica"]
        Med4["Biossensores"]
        Med5["Engenharia Tecidual"]
    end

    subgraph Eletronica["Eletrônica"]
        Elec1["Transistores Nanoscópicos"]
        Elec2["Memórias de Alta Densidade"]
        Elec3["Displays Flexíveis"]
        Elec4["Circuitos Quânticos"]
    end

    subgraph Energia["Energia"]
        Energ1["Células Solares"]
        Energ2["Baterias de Alta Capacidade"]
        Energ3["Hidrogênio Armazenamento"]
        Energ4["Super Capacitores"]
    end

    subgraph Ambiente["Meio Ambiente"]
        Amb1["Filtragem de Água"]
        Amb2["Remediação de Solo"]
        Amb3["Detecção de Poluentes"]
        Amb4["Catálise Verde"]
    end

    subgraph Industria["Indústria"]
        Ind1["Materiais Compósitos"]
        Ind2["Revestimentos Protetores"]
        Ind3["Lubrificantes Avançados"]
        Ind4["Têxteis Inteligentes"]
    end

    Applications --> Medicina
    Applications --> Eletronica
    Applications --> Energia
    Applications --> Ambiente
    Applications --> Industria

    %% Desafios e considerações
    Medicina --> Challenges["Desafios e Considerações"]
    Eletronica --> Challenges
    Energia --> Challenges
    Ambiente --> Challenges
    Industria --> Challenges

    Challenges --> Challenge1["Segurança e Toxicidade"]
    Challenges --> Challenge2["Regulamentação"]
    Challenges --> Challenge3["Ética"]
    Challenges --> Challenge4["Custo de Produção"]
    Challenges --> Challenge5["Escala Industrial"]

    %% Futuro
    Challenges --> Future["Futuro da Nanotecnologia"]
    Future --> Fut1["Nanomedicina Personalizada"]
    Future --> Fut2["Computação Quântica"]
    Future --> Fut3["Nanofábricas Moleculares"]
    Future --> Fut4["Novos Materiais"]

    %% Estilização
    classDef mainStyle fill:#2E86AB,stroke:#023E8A,color:#fff,stroke-width:3px
    classDef scaleStyle fill:#A23B72,stroke:#721c45,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px
    classDef propStyle fill:#F18F01,stroke:#c25500,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px
    classDef methodStyle fill:#C73E1D,stroke:#8a260e,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px
    classDef materialStyle fill:#3B1F2B,stroke:#2a1520,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px
    classDef appStyle fill:#6B705C,stroke:#3d4036,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px
    classDef challengeStyle fill:#D08C60,stroke:#9c5c36,color:#fff,stroke-width:2px
    classDef futureStyle fill:#56CFE1,stroke:#2a9db5,color:#1a3a40,stroke-width:2px

    class Start mainStyle
    class Scale,NanoScale,Atomic,Micro scaleStyle
    class Properties,Prop1,Prop2,Prop3,Prop4,Prop5 propStyle
    class Methods,TopDown,BottomUp methodStyle
    class Materials,Mat1,Mat2,Mat3,Mat4,Mat5 materialStyle
    class Applications,Medicina,Eletronica,Energia,Ambiente,Industria appStyle
    class Challenges,Challenge1,Challenge2,Challenge3,Challenge4,Challenge5 challengeStyle
    class Future,Fut1,Fut2,Fut3,Fut4 futureStyle
